response: {"gender":"Male","age":"28","decision_strategy":"I decided which image was luckier by trying to remember if an image was worth Money vs just being Neutral from the second part. If I didn't remember seeing one image or the other, or if I couldn't remember which value it was assigned, I just tried to think about the activity itself and compared it with other similar activities that were either worth Money or Neutral, and then I made my decision based on that.","consistency_check":"For the most part, yes, but not always. Like I mentioned above, if I was sure about the value (Money vs Neutral) of one image or the other I picked the Money one as being more lucky every time. But if I couldn't remember seeing one or both of the images in part 2 or if I couldn't remember their value(s) from part 2, I compared the action itself (physically performing an action vs spending money vs doing something useful vs etc.) to a similar action which I did remember the value of and then did the same thing (Money = Luckier, so I should choose that one / Neutral = Not Lucky, so I should choose the other one instead in hopes of it being Lucky)."}
